source: War contribution floating of local loan__CO273:661:5
filename: War contribution floating of local loan__CO273:661:5.txt
summary:
- **Core topic/purpose:** British Colonial Office correspondence and legislation on **raising local war loans in Malaya** (Straits Settlements and Federated Malay States) whose proceeds would be **given to His Majesty’s Government/UK Treasury** as a **war contribution** during WWII.  
- **Geography/time period:** **Malaya (Singapore/Straits Settlements; Federated Malay States/Kuala Lumpur)**, mainly **May–Dec 1940** (with loan redemption provisions extending to the 1950s).  
- **Major proposals/debates:** Ongoing dispute over whether Malaya’s war contribution should be financed by **income tax** (advocated by Governor **Sir Shenton Thomas**) versus a **public loan** (promoted in local press, notably the **Straits Times**, and supported by some unofficial members). Concerns raised about post-war slumps (rubber/tin) affecting ability to service debt.  
- **Key decisions/approvals:** The Secretary of State/Colonial Office indicates willingness to accept a **loan-based contribution** if there is substantial unofficial support, with attention to **servicing arrangements** and **amortisation** (discussion includes a suggested ~**15-year** amortisation, though enacted terms differ). Colonial Office approves proceeding with **covering legislation** for loans and (in the Straits Settlements) **war savings certificates**, while cautioning against forcing income tax via official majorities.  
- **Federated Malay States legislation (Enactment No. 16 of 1940):** Authorizes the High Commissioner to raise up to **$20,000,000** via **3%** bearer-bond loan (“**F.M.S. War Loan (1940)**”), proceeds to be **presented to the UK Government**; includes a **sinking fund** of at least **2% annually**; redeemable **not earlier than 1 Oct 1952** and **not later than 30 Sep 1959**; passed **20 Aug 1940**, assented by FMS rulers; reported to Colonial Office (Legal Adviser report dated **13 Sep 1940**).  
- **Straits Settlements legislation (Aug 1940):**  
  - **War Loan (Local) Ordinance, 1940 (No. 32):** authorizes a loan up to **$25,000,000**, **3%**, debentures, redeemable **1952–1959** window; sinking fund contributions (not less than **2%** each half-year) remitted to **Crown Agents** for investment.  
  - **War Savings Certificates Ordinance, 1940 (No. 33):** authorizes up to **$10,000,000** via Post Office certificates; **$15** unit repaid as **$20 after 10 years** (stated as **3% compound**); early redemption after 1 year with interest (≤3% compound); sinking fund formula specified.  
- **Key actors and money flows/publicity:** Actors include the **Colonial Office**, **Treasury**, **Crown Agents**, the **High Commissioner** (S. W. Jones), the **Officer Administering the Government** (Straits Settlements), and local councils/committees. Proceeds remitted to Crown Agents and paid to **H.M. Treasury as “free unconditional gift”**; reported totals include **£2,932,942 14s. 2d.** (Straits Settlements) and **~£2.3–£2.5 million** (FMS; figures vary in telegrams), with press/PR discussion over whether funds were intended specifically for **aircraft** (e.g., Times report citing “150 bombers or 300 Hurricanes/Spitfires”), ultimately treated as general war contribution unless explicitly earmarked.
